content:
Also, Looks like the OCPN plugin manual needs some update..
Some plugins are in main and beta lists.
Some plugins are available in the main catalog but not listed with the "up arrow" (not intuitive since the up arrow is supposed to indicate "included" and not "available" plugins as it says at the beginning).
Some plugins are completely missing.

Plugin list is not up-to-date between the main website and the plugin's manual :
https://opencpn.org/OpenCPN/info/downloadplugins.html
https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/opencpn-plugins/index-main.html
And I believe the list from the website is the most used/ easily accessible for users.

And I believe the list from the website is the most used/ easily accessible for users.

Probably not. The actual list used by users is the catalog, displayed by opencpn under Options | Plugins. The primary website(s) use case is that in this list plugins link to them under "More info"
... that is not to say it should not be corrected. Patches welcome...
3 messages were moved here from #Master > Demo plugin by Alec Leamas.

Probably not. The actual list used by users is the catalog, displayed by opencpn under Options | Plugins.

I meant between the plugins listed in the website and the plugin's manual list.
Well... patches welcome.

The webiste is not always maintained, agreed.

Everything else is in git. Note that the actual contents lives in the plugins' repos. The simple way to update this is to use the "Edit this page" link in the manual pages.

If a plugin is missing in the plugins manual please raise an issue at https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/main. For this to be meaningful the plugion should have an updated manual/ directory

If a plugin is missing in the plugins manual please raise an issue at https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/main. For this to be meaningful the plugion should have an updated manual/ directory

Alec, I am ready to revise and improve the TestPlugin Manual , however I do not find it anymore and I don't appear to have access to make those changes.

I do see that you have moved the OpenCPN manual to https://github.com/OpenCPN/OpenCPN/blob/master/manual/modules/ROOT/nav.adoc from the original opencpn-manuals repo and this is what appears here in the manual website .

The manual section I am looking for and cannot find is the Dev Plugin Compile section, it was never located under "Testplugin_pi". It was a part of opencpn-manuals.

I think I found it here https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/ocpn-dev-manual/0.1/index.html and the menus seem to go in circles and I never find the Testplugin Developer documents. I was hopeful to find them from this location, https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/ocpn-dev-manual/0.1/index.html but non of the links lead to that section.

Also I am logged in and there is no Edit button. Why would that be?

Do you know how I can access those pages so that I can edit them?

Thanks
Rick

Ok I found the Plugin Dev Manual, it is in a different repo here, https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/tree/main/modules/ROOT/pages

The reason I know that is because of pages like these:
https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/blob/main/modules/ROOT/pages/pm-tp-system-structure.adoc
https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/blob/main/modules/ROOT/pages/pm-overview-prereq-services.adoc
https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/blob/main/modules/ROOT/pages/pm-tp-dev-setup.adoc

Why aren't these pages showing in the menu system? Or at least I can't get there after lots of tries, although there are circular references and not much consistency in the side menu.

Don't we want to have a link to the basics about compiling plugins?

@Alec Leamas Ok now I managed to find a webpage link somehow (don't ask how)

https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/ocpn-dev-manual/0.1/pm-tp-template.html

but it does not allow me to edit.

The "Edit this Page" exists! I had to go to "Maximize" Browser.
Now I see that this is all part of

OpenCPN Developer Manual
https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/ocpn-dev-manual/0.1/index.html

Does this link have a spot in the OpenCPN Dev Menu? What is the structure here? I can't get to it from anywhere sensible. It essentially is not public.

Why aren't these pages showing in the menu system?

The menu hierarchy top is nav.adoc. Anything ther should be visible in the left pane menu.

However, here is some strange error I havn't had time to hunt down: In a local build all menu items are visible, but not in the published site. It's not just about testplugin, all sorts of links are are missing.
Thanks Alec for the clarification, after I get more comfortable again with asciidoc, I will try to fix the menus, but I will first clean up TP docs and move them to Testplugin similar to SD.

Always the one to have the contrary view.

  1. No one reads manuals.
  2. Few contribute to writing documentation (kudos to Rick for corralling some of us to contribute to the user manual.)
  3. The current manual is incomplete, sections yet to be written, circular references, isolated chapters and scattered between GitHub, gitlab, gist, OpenCPN.org

You would need a whiteboard to sketch all of the buckets of documentation and the links (or lack thereof) to understand what goes where.

I propose that instead of making a rod for our backs, delete most of it.

At the moment we direct aspiring plugin developers to incomplete, incomprehensible or even worse, incorrect information.

Less is more.

Re, Alec's observation with Antora, I noticed a problem either in antora-playbook.yml or antora.yml, a difference in relative paths for local vs GitHub processing.

@Rick Gleason

The missing links in the left pane seems resurrected. I'm not quite sure why this was resolved, one possible reason might be buggy GUI build invocation (current update is done in the CLI)

@TwoCan

You would need a whiteboard to sketch all of the buckets of documentation and the links (or lack thereof) to understand what goes where.

You? We?

No one reads manuals.

Partly true. However, they have important use cases. The most obvious is users googling or using AI to get some answers. The manuals often pops up in these replies.

Another use case is when assisting users. Pointing to a manual page saves tons of time when the alternative is to manually guide users step by step.

Bottom line: we need manuals. But since no one reads them, the lack of overall organization is a less pressing problem.

Few contribute to writing documentation

Yes. What we can do is to move the manual sources close to the developers. Writing docs when you implement new functions is natural. This is why plugin manuals sources now are in the plugins, and the developer manual is part of main opencpn.

Less is more.

Agreed. I will happily merge changes removing outdated, useless stuff from the developer manual. As for the user manual, this is Rick's show and he is more reluctant to remove things. One reason is that since the user manual is not in git removed things are lost.

Partly true. However, they have important use cases. The most obvious is users googling or using AI to get some answers. The manuals often pops up in these replies.

Another use case is when assisting users. Pointing to a manual page saves tons of time when the alternative is to manually guide users step by step.

Bottom line: we need manuals. But since no one reads them, the lack of overall organization is a less pressing problem.

I totally agree. They feed AI searches with good information.
Ability to point to a manual saves time and hopefully provides an answer.
I hope to improve the situation for Dev Testplugin documentation and organization.
I will try to keep it brief, but there are many moving parts for Managed Plugins.

Alec thanks for fixing the menus. Now getting back into asciidoc/antora. I can do this now!
TwoCan we know you are a cantankerous cout, but a lovable one who has points.

As for the user manual, this is Rick's show and he is more reluctant to remove things. One reason is that since the user manual is not in git removed things are lost.

are you talking about this manual : https://opencpn.org/wiki/dokuwiki/doku.php?id=opencpn ?
There is a "see old revisions" button on the right so nothing is really lost ? But I agree this is less convenient than git.

What about moving this to a github repo and using ascii/antora too ?
Would make it easier to manage, contribute, etc.
Yes. But so far Rick is not convinced
@Rick Gleason ^^

That link goes nowhere Kupofty but I think I know where you pointed, the website seems very slow this morning.

Well, that is not a simple task. Conversion is not easy and I find that screenshots and images are not handled very well in asciidoc/antora, (although I am getting used to it now). Additionally to "view" the end result you have to run antora site.yml and then view the actual page in a browser. This is cumbersome when editing as compared to docuwiki.

I've been using Visual Studio to edit and that is helpful as the finished page can be viewed sans images and screenshots. So the issue about cumbersome adding of screenshots and images and viewing with screenshots is still there.

Perhaps there are now some tools developed for sane handling of images and viewing of pages with screenshot that I am not aware of.

Also Pavel has made a conversion of the Quick Start Guide (which may not be entirely up to date). It is about 25 mb and is used in the downloadable manual plugin. The current full wiki is quite large. The data folder is about 2.5 gb. I think the quick start guide is a very good standin for the full wiki.

That link goes nowhere Kupofty but I think I know where you pointed, the website seems very slow this morning.

Works for me now. It was the link for "OpenCPN Manuals" table of contents

I find that screenshots and images are not handled very well in asciidoc/antora, (although I am getting used to it now).

Did not encounter any issue with images for my manual. What problems did you face ?

Additionally to "view" the end result you have to run antora site.yml and then view the actual page in a browser. This is cumbersome when editing as compared to docuwiki.

You can also live render the view in VSCode (except for pictures).

Perhaps there are now some tools developed for sane handling of images and viewing of pages with screenshot that I am not aware of.

Will take another look.

Also Pavel has made a conversion of the Quick Start Guide (which may not be entirely up to date). It is about 25 mb and is used in the downloadable manual plugin.

HTML file won't open on Linux. Will retry on Windows when I have access to my PC.

The data folder is about 2.5 gb.

What makes it so large ? Pictures ? Video ?
Yes that is what I meant . "You can also live render the view in VSCode (except for pictures)."
No video, it is screenshots.

I checked an old 2024 local version, and there are a number of folders, including cache, index, log, media, media-meta, meta, attic, media-attic, etc. but, the ones we are probably most interested in are index (1.3mb), media (251 mb), pages (2.6 mb).

I don't think the repository site is a problem here. I don't remember the details, but when we moved the developer and plugins manual out of mediawiki the wiki data was really large. However, the antora manuals are quite reasonable. Probably same thing here, mediawiki just uses a lot of space.

From a helicopter perspective this seems to be about convenience when editing vs the overall Antora advantages I think we all see. Part of the "convenience" problem is that Antora/Asciidoc is more or less new to Rick and others, and changing tools is always cumbersome.

Also, a possible move of the user manual into Antora is a huge undertaking involving lots of manual hands on. Nothing like that will happen now when the sailing season starts for most of us.

@Rick Gleason You mentioned you were about to move the testplugin docs out of the main developer manual to a separate repo like shipdriver. I think this is where to start. It's a good move, and you'll encounter the wonders of Antora and Asciidoc in more detail in the process.

Let's postpone possible discussions about the user manual format at least until the sailing season ends.

Alec it is actually Dokuwiki, but yes, lets make this move at the end of the sailing season please.
I will work on Testplugin docs to move them out and update them. Thanks.
